solution: 
```cpp
class Solution {
public:
    long long minEnd(int n, int x) {
        long long res = x;
        for (int i = 1; i < n; ++i) {
            res = (res << 1) | (res & 1);
            if ((res & x) != x) {
                res |= 1;
            }
        }
        return res;
    }
};
```
efficiency: MEDIUM
time complexity: O(n)
space complexity: O(1)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: <The solution constructs the array by shifting and setting bits to ensure the AND result is x. It iteratively builds the minimum possible array. The main strength is its simplicity, but the linear time complexity may be a weakness for large n.>
